Author: Don Lim
Title: Vibe Coding vs. Cursor AI: Senior Dev’s UNFILTERED Assessment
Text:
Member-only story
Vibe Coding vs. Cursor AI: Senior Dev’s UNFILTERED Assessment
If you are a non-coder, go for Manus or Lovable 2. If you are a coder, Cursor AI tops Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, and Firebase Studio.
For non-coders:
As of April 2025, in my opinion, Manus AI has the greatest number of versatile tools and comprehensive task planning. It might offer the best way to build an app for personal purposes at this time. Lovable 2, which came out this week, can be a great tool for vibe coders. There are other options, but many of them don’t let you host the app with a URL that you can share. By the time other vendors catch up to the present level of Manus and Lovable 2, these front-runners will probably come up with more stable services and keep their status in the foreseeable future.
For coders:
If you are a coder, Cursor AI tops Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot (Microsoft), and Firebase Studio (Google). I tried all of them to a certain extent in the past few weeks as soon as their new updates came out. For me, $20 a month for Cursor AI, powered by Claude 3.7 Sonnet Thinking, prevails over all other AI-assisted coding tools. There are tools like Replit, Bolt.new, and Windsurf, but they are a little bit behind Cursor AI in my…
